accepted.chapter_03_pass_02.json|question|3-8-2|0: Distinct maneuver boundary: stop-and-go changes status at a complete stop, while the live item tests touch-and-go at touchdown.
accepted.chapter_04_pass_02.json|question|4-5-4|1: Distinct table band: 29.50 inches requires a different lowest usable flight level than the live 29.92-or-higher item.
accepted.chapter_07_pass_02.json|flashcard|7-8-2|: Complete four-part Class C service decomposition; the older card compresses the service and omits the IFR/VFR separation distinction.
accepted.chapter_08_pass_02.json|question|8-2-1|0: Intentional threshold contrast: the staged two-minute revision is below the forwarding threshold, while the live four-minute case exceeds it.
accepted.chapter_09_pass_02.json|question|9-7-2|0: Synthesis retrieval: combines authorization, separation, and safety-condition duties that live items test only in isolation.
accepted.chapter_10_pass_02.json|question|10-2-11: Cross-element synthesis: retrieves both required technical-assistance information and the prohibition against advising bomb-disposal action.
accepted.chapter_10_pass_02.json|question|10-2-15: Complete list retrieval of all seven emergency-airport factors rather than recognition of one factor.
